preamble: The voiceless velar fricative [x] can undergo vocalization, a process where a consonant transforms into a vowel. In specific phonological environments, this vocalized [x] can then combine with an adjacent vowel to form a diphthong. This shift is notably attested in the constructed language Sindarin, where the fricative lenites to a vowel, leading to diphthongization.

examples.note: The voiceless velar fricative [x] vocalizes to [i] between a vowel and [θ], forming the diphthong [ai].

sources: Eldamo: Sindarin: [x], [ɸ] vocalized between a vowel and [θ].
